Container.Name: bluesky-crosspost
Container.Overview: Automatically cross-post Bluesky posts to Telegram and Discord with a web-based management interface.
Container.Changes: ### 2026-03-17 - Added web-based configuration UI - Password encryption support - Unified /config mount for all data - Support for generic Docker and Unraid deployments

Variable.Name: CONFIG_DIR
Variable.Description: Path for storing configuration, logs, and data files

Variable.Name: LOG_LEVEL
Variable.Description: Logging level: DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR

Variable.Name: BLUESKY_PASSWORD
Variable.Description: Your Bluesky app password (NOT your login password!)

Variable.Name: BLUESKY_TARGET_HANDLE
Variable.Description: Account to monitor for posts (can be same as BLUESKY_HANDLE)

Variable.Name: BLUESKY_CHECK_INTERVAL
Variable.Description: How often to check for new posts (seconds)

Variable.Name: TELEGRAM_CHANNEL_ID
Variable.Description: Telegram channel ID to post to

Variable.Name: DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN
Variable.Description: Discord bot token from Developer Portal

Variable.Name: DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID
Variable.Description: Discord channel ID to post to

Variable.Name: ADMIN_PASSWORD
Variable.Description: Password for web interface login (will be encrypted)

Container.DonateText: If you find this useful, consider supporting the developer
Container.Description:
Automatically cross-post Bluesky posts to Telegram and Discord channels with a beautiful web-based configuration interface.
**Features:** - 🚀 Auto-monitor Bluesky account and cross-post to Telegram & Discord - 🌐 Web-based UI for easy configuration - 🔐 Encrypted password storage - 📊 Activity dashboard with post history - 📋 Real-time system logs - 🔄 One-click restart with settings sync
**Quick Start:** 1. Set your Bluesky handle and app password 2. (Optional) Add Telegram bot token and channel ID 3. (Optional) Add Discord bot token and channel ID 4. Access web UI at https://[your-ip]:2759 5. Login with admin/admin (change password in web UI)
**Notes:** - Use an **app password** from Bluesky Settings, NOT your login password - Get Telegram bot token from @BotFather on Telegram - Get Discord bot token from Discord Developer Portal - All configuration can be done through the web UI - Passwords are encrypted before being stored - Logs stored in /mnt/user/appdata/bluesky-crosspost/logs/
